message:
docs: improve beads-release formula with lessons learned

Add robustness improvements based on v0.42.0 release experience:

- Add preflight-sync step to catch beads issues early
- Split local-install into install + verify-local-install gate
- Add dev build instructions (go build + cp to ~/.local/bin)
- Improve daemon restart with version verification
- Add post-release-sync step for clean state
- Add verification checklist to release-complete step

Formula version bumped to 2.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {
   "formula": "beads-release",
   "type": "workflow",
-  "description": "Beads release workflow - from version bump to verified release.

This formula orchestrates a complete release cycle:
1. Preflight checks (clean git, up to date)
2. Documentation updates (CHANGELOG, info.go)
3. Version bump (all components)
4. Git operations (commit, tag, push)
5. CI verification (GitHub Actions)
6. Artifact verification (GitHub, npm, PyPI)
7. Local installation update
8. Daemon restart

## Usage

```bash
bd wisp create beads-release --var version=0.37.0
```

Or assign to a polecat:
```bash
gt sling beads/polecats/p1 --formula beads-release --var version=0.37.0
```",
-  "version": 1,
+  "description": "Beads release workflow - from version bump to verified release.

This formula orchestrates a complete release cycle:
1. Preflight checks (clean git, synced beads, up to date)
2. Documentation updates (CHANGELOG, info.go)
3. Version bump (all components)
4. Git operations (commit, tag, push)
5. CI verification (GitHub Actions)
6. Artifact verification (GitHub, npm, PyPI)
7. Local installation update (with verification gate)
8. Daemon restart (with version verification)
9. Post-release cleanup

## Usage

```bash
bd wisp create beads-release --var version=0.37.0
```

Or assign to a polecat:
```bash
gt sling beads/polecats/p1 --formula beads-release --var version=0.37.0
```",
+  "version": 2,
   "vars": {
     "version": {
       "description": "The semantic version to release (e.g., 0.37.0)",
@@ -15,11 +15,17 @@
       "title": "Preflight: Check git status",
       "description": "Ensure working tree is clean before starting release.

```bash
git status
```

If there are uncommitted changes, either:
- Commit them first
- Stash them: `git stash`
- Abort and resolve"
     },
+    {
+      "id": "preflight-sync",
+      "title": "Preflight: Sync beads",
+      "description": "Ensure beads are synced before starting release.

```bash
bd sync
```

This catches:
- Stale worktrees that need repair
- Uncommitted beads changes in other clones
- Divergence issues that would block later syncs

If sync fails, resolve the issue before proceeding.",

+    },

       "title": "Update local installation",
-      "description": "Update local bd to the new version.

Option 1 - Homebrew:
```bash
brew upgrade bd
```

Option 2 - Install script:
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steveyegge/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
```

Verify:
```bash

```

Should show {{version}}.",
+      "description": "Update local bd to the new version.

**Detect install method:**
```bash
which bd
```

**Option 1 - Dev build** (if bd is in ~/.local/bin or project dir):
```bash
go build -o bd ./cmd/bd
cp ./bd ~/.local/bin/bd
```

**Option 2 - Homebrew** (if bd is in /opt/homebrew or /usr/local):
```bash
brew upgrade bd
```

**Option 3 - Install script:**
```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/steveyegge/beads/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
```",
       "needs": ["verify-npm", "verify-pypi"]
     },
+    {
+      "id": "verify-local-install",
+      "title": "Verify local installation",
+      "description": "**GATE: This step must pass before proceeding.**

Verify the installed version matches the release:

```bash
bd --version
```

**Expected output:** `bd version {{version}}`

**If version mismatch:**
1. Check which bd is in PATH: `which bd`
2. Rebuild if dev install: `go build -o bd ./cmd/bd && cp ./bd ~/.local/bin/bd`
3. Verify again: `bd --version`

**Do not proceed until `bd --version` shows {{version}}.**",
+      "needs": ["local-install"]
+    },
     {
       "id": "restart-daemons",
       "title": "Restart daemons",
-      "description": "Restart bd daemons to pick up new version.

```bash
bd daemons killall
```

Daemons will auto-restart with new version on next bd command.

Verify:
```bash
bd daemons list
```",
-      "needs": ["local-install"]
+      "description": "Restart bd daemons to pick up new version.

```bash
bd daemons killall
```

Trigger daemon restart:
```bash
bd list >/dev/null 2>&1
```

Verify daemon version:
```bash
bd daemons list
```

**Expected:** All daemons should show version {{version}}.

If daemons show old version, they may be running from a different bd binary.
Check with: `ps aux | grep bd.*daemon`",
+      "needs": ["verify-local-install"]
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "post-release-sync",
+      "title": "Post-release: Sync beads",
+      "description": "Sync beads to capture any changes made during release.

```bash
bd sync
```

This ensures:
- Molecule progress is committed
- Any beads updates are pushed
- Clean state for next session",
+      "needs": ["restart-daemons"]
     },
     {
       "id": "release-complete",
       "title": "Release complete",
-      "description": "Release v{{version}} is complete!

Summary:
- All version files updated
- Git tag pushed
- CI artifacts built
- npm and PyPI packages published
- Local installation updated
- Daemons restarted

Optional next steps:
- Announce on social media
- Update documentation site
- Close related milestone",
-      "needs": ["restart-daemons"]
+      "description": "Release v{{version}} is complete!

**Verification checklist:**
- [ ] `bd --version` shows {{version}}
- [ ] `bd daemons list` shows {{version}}
- [ ] GitHub release has all artifacts
- [ ] npm package is {{version}}
- [ ] PyPI package is {{version}}
- [ ] `bd sync` succeeded

**Summary:**
- All version files updated
- Git tag pushed
- CI artifacts built
- npm and PyPI packages published
- Local installation verified
- Daemons restarted with new version
- Beads synced

**Note:** If this was a wisp, it will be squashed to a digest.
The hook should clear automatically after squash.",
+      "needs": ["post-release-sync"]
     }
   ]
 }

 {"id":"bd-hobo","title":"Distinct prefixes for protos, molecules, wisps","description":"Template/workflow entities should have visually distinct prefixes from regular issues.

**Problem:**
- Protos (bd-7bs4) look like regular issues - invites squashing
- Molecules (poured instances) also use bd- prefix
- Wisps are in separate DB but still use bd- when referenced

**Proposed Prefixes:**
- `proto-` for templates (e.g., proto-release, proto-review)
- `mol-` for active molecules (poured from protos)
- `wisp-` for ephemeral wisps (vapor phase)

**Benefits:**
- Instant visual recognition of entity type
- Prevents accidental modification of templates
- Clear lifecycle: proto → mol → wisp → digest

**Implementation options:**
1. Separate databases with different prefixes
2. Issue type determines prefix generation
3. Naming convention enforced by bd pour/wisp commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T16:45:24.940809-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","labels":["workflow"]}
-{"id":"bd-hp8g","title":"gt sling should look up formulas from .beads/formulas/ registry","description":"## Problem

gt sling <formula-name> doesn't find formulas from the registry that bd formula list shows.

## What I tried

1. gt sling beads/crew/emma --formula beads-release --var version=0.42.0
   - Problem: --formula flag doesn't exist

2. gt sling beads-release beads/crew/emma --var version=0.42.0
   - Error: open .../beads-release: no such file or directory
   - Problem: Looks for file in cwd, not formula registry

## What worked

bd mol wisp beads-release --var version=0.42.0 - correctly finds formula from .beads/formulas/

## Expected

gt sling should use the same formula lookup as bd mol wisp - checking .beads/formulas/*.formula.json

## Documentation gap

The help shows examples like 'gt sling mol-release mayor/' suggesting formula slinging works, but it doesn't find registry formulas.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/jade","created_at":"2025-12-30T21:18:17.251292-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-30T21:57:10.487081-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-hp8g","title":"gt sling should look up formulas from .beads/formulas/ registry","description":"## Problem

gt sling <formula-name> doesn't find formulas from the registry that bd formula list shows.

## What I tried

1. gt sling beads/crew/emma --formula beads-release --var version=0.42.0
   - Problem: --formula flag doesn't exist

2. gt sling beads-release beads/crew/emma --var version=0.42.0
   - Error: open .../beads-release: no such file or directory
   - Problem: Looks for file in cwd, not formula registry

## What worked

bd mol wisp beads-release --var version=0.42.0 - correctly finds formula from .beads/formulas/

## Expected

gt sling should use the same formula lookup as bd mol wisp - checking .beads/formulas/*.formula.json

## Documentation gap

The help shows examples like 'gt sling mol-release mayor/' suggesting formula slinging works, but it doesn't find registry formulas.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"beads/polecats/jade","created_at":"2025-12-30T21:18:17.251292-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:06:27.153176-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T22:06:27.153176-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed in both repos: bd cook now looks up formulas from registry, and gt sling uses bd mol wisp"}
 {"id":"bd-hr39","title":"bd cook: needs field not converted to step dependencies","description":"When cooking a formula with `needs` fields on steps, the dependencies are not created between sibling steps.

## Expected

Formula:
```yaml
steps:
  - id: inbox-check
    title: Check inbox
  - id: trigger-spawns
    title: Nudge polecats
    needs: [inbox-check]
```

Should create:
- mol-foo.inbox-check (no deps)
- mol-foo.trigger-spawns → depends on → mol-foo.inbox-check

## Actual

Both steps only depend on the parent proto:
- mol-foo.inbox-check → depends on → mol-foo
- mol-foo.trigger-spawns → depends on → mol-foo

The `needs` field is ignored.

## Impact

This breaks the step execution order. Steps that should wait for predecessors will run in parallel or out of order.

## Reproduction

```bash
bd cook mol-deacon-patrol.formula.yaml
bd show mol-deacon-patrol.trigger-pending-spawns
# Shows: Depends on mol-deacon-patrol (parent only)
# Should show: Depends on mol-deacon-patrol.inbox-check
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:49:59.023514-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.929298-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T13:59:09.929298-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-hulf","title":"Molecule execution state management","description":"Implement molecule execution state tracking.

## State Location

```
.beads/molecules/<mol-id>.state.yaml
```

## State Schema

```yaml
id: mol-deacon-patrol
formula: mol-deacon-patrol  # Source formula
bonded_at: ISO timestamp
bonded_by: entity who created it

# Execution state
status: running | paused | completed | failed
current_step: step-id
started_at: ISO timestamp
completed_at: ISO timestamp (if done)

# Loop tracking
reset_count: 0
last_reset_at: null

# Per-step state
steps:
  inbox-check:
    status: completed | in_progress | pending
    started_at: ...
    completed_at: ...
  spawn-work:
    status: pending
  self-inspect:
    status: pending

# Variables (from formula instantiation)
variables:
  rig: gastown
  issue_id: gt-xxx
```

## Operations

- CreateState(molID, formula, variables) - Initialize state
- LoadState(molID) - Read current state  
- SaveState(molID, state) - Write state
- AdvanceStep(molID) - Mark current complete, find next
- ResetState(molID) - Reset all steps to pending

## Files

- internal/mol/state.go
- internal/mol/types.go","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T15:53:43.381634-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T16:53:11.807645-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T16:53:11.807645-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-hvng","title":"Merge: bd-w193","description":"branch: polecat/nux
target: main
source_issue: bd-w193
rig: beads","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"merge-request","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:47.496139-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T23:17:26.996479-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T23:17:26.996479-08:00"}
@@ -829,6 +829,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-x3hi","title":"Support redirect files in .beads/ directory","description":"Gas Town creates polecat worktrees with .beads/redirect files that point to a shared beads database. The bd CLI should:

1. When finding a .beads/ directory, check if it contains a 'redirect' file
2. If redirect exists, read the relative path and use that as the beads directory
3. This allows multiple git worktrees to share a single beads database

Example:
- polecats/alpha/.beads/redirect contains '../../mayor/rig/.beads'
- bd commands from alpha should use mayor/rig/.beads

Currently bd ignores redirect files and either uses the local .beads/ or walks up to find a parent .beads/.

Related: gt-nriy (test message that can't be retrieved due to missing redirect support)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:46:23.415172-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:59:25.759664-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T21:59:25.759664-08:00"}
